title: ficus citrifolia subsp. brevifolia
intro: Ficus citrifolia subsp. brevifolia, also known as the 'Banyan fig', is a subspecies of the Ficus citrifolia tree, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, particularly in Hawaii. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the Ficus citrifolia subsp. brevifolia are used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be taken to alleviate various health conditions.
